divisions.purpose: Define direction, guard scope, and coordinate command-level oversight.
escalation_requirements: Escalate any ambiguity affecting scope, authority, or safety.

divisions.purpose: Capture incoming work, normalize requests, and map intent into structured records.
escalation_requirements: Escalate missing context, contradictory instructions, or unsafe requests.

divisions.purpose: Convert requirements into staged plans, sequences, and strategic options.
escalation_requirements: Escalate when tradeoffs change scope, schedule, or risk exposure.

divisions.purpose: Collect, organize, and synthesize knowledge into static reference material.
escalation_requirements: Escalate when source quality, freshness, or reliability is uncertain.

divisions.purpose: Design code changes, build artifacts, and implementation plans without live execution.
escalation_requirements: Escalate changes affecting runtime, deployment, or protected baselines.

divisions.purpose: Check correctness, regressions, and conformance against static requirements.
escalation_requirements: Escalate when a defect might affect safety, data integrity, or production risk.

divisions.purpose: Produce clear records, reports, and user-facing documentation.
escalation_requirements: Escalate when wording could imply activation, authority, or capability that does not exist.

divisions.purpose: Define defensive boundaries, restrictions, and safety checks for the worker blueprint.
escalation_requirements: Escalate any condition that could expose secrets, credentials, or production systems.

divisions.purpose: Plan operational readiness, release gating, and non-executing deployment support.
escalation_requirements: Escalate any request involving deployment, runtime drift, or production activation.

divisions.purpose: Frame product intent, business goals, and user value in static planning form.
escalation_requirements: Escalate if business objectives conflict with safety, compliance, or capacity limits.

divisions.purpose: Develop static creative direction, media concepts, and presentation assets.
escalation_requirements: Escalate when a creative request crosses into live publishing or rights-sensitive use.

divisions.purpose: Describe measurement, reporting, and analytic structure without live query access.
escalation_requirements: Escalate any request that implies direct data access, credentialed access, or production telemetry use.

divisions.purpose: Draft support patterns, personalization logic, and user assistance guidance.
escalation_requirements: Escalate when a support response would require live account, credential, or production access.

divisions.purpose: Track policy, compliance, and audit-ready records for the static worker system.
escalation_requirements: Escalate any policy exception, audit failure, or control gap.

divisions.purpose: Prototype future static structures and sandboxed conceptual variants without activation.
escalation_requirements: Escalate any experiment that could be mistaken for live execution or deployment.

capacity_phases.notes: Planning and design only; no activation or live orchestration.
Planning and design only; no activation or live orchestration.
Planning and design only; no activation or live orchestration.
Final planning target only; still static design with no execution.

permission_tiers.description: Design-only records with no execution permissions.
Local planning only, with no live actions.
Future drafting tier reserved for later design stages.
Future validation tier reserved for later design stages.
Future sandbox execution tier reserved for later design stages.
Future supervised tool-use tier reserved for later design stages.
Future supervised API-preview tier reserved for later design stages.
Future supervised production-candidate tier reserved for later design stages.
Future production pilot tier reserved for later design stages.
Future locked live tier reserved for later design stages.

activation_tiers.description: Worker record does not exist yet.
Registered template with no live activity.

audit_levels.description: Minimal design note with no operational record.
Basic structured record for static design.
Structured record of capabilities and limits.
Role record that requires validation evidence.
Role record that requires approval review.
Comprehensive audit trail for later phases.
